resources.string: A responsive personal portfolio built with Compose Multiplatform, featuring dynamic content and smooth animations.
Cross-platform task management app with offline support and sync.
Lightweight API gateway with authentication and rate limiting.
Writing software daily, for fun and growth

For work, I build whatever’s needed at the best value I can provide. I was trained in Android development when the timing was right, but over the years I’ve come to feel confident working in just about any modern language or stack.

What I do best is taking the abstract ideas people bring me — the picture they have in their heads of how a project should look or feel — and turning that into something concrete. I focus on refining the connections, making sure nothing important is missed, and delivering something complete and functional.

Professionally, most of my experience is with large-scale retail mobile applications. In my personal time, I work on whatever I’m motivated to finish, almost always in Kotlin. I’m very results-oriented: I’d rather focus on projects that will be completed than sink time into something that won’t reach the finish line.

I think a lot about how life is fleeting and how limited our time is, especially in the years when we’re strongest. What keeps me moving are the people I know who are also wearing their own masks but still accomplish the things I wish I could. They remind me that it’s possible.

Most of what I do — in lifting, in projects, in life — comes down to faith that I’m not some special snowflake who can’t grow. If I keep at it, even slowly, the small progress I’ve seen so far will keep adding up. Slowing down or stopping always makes things worse; going at a reasonable speed keeps me steady.

When I finally let go of worry and realize there’s nothing urgent to stress over, I find relief in small things — petting my animals, meditating, or just breathing for a moment. I don’t expect anyone to waste empathy on me; I just want to give a bit of context about the person behind the code. This is me trying, moving forward, one step at a time.
Paint your own masterpiece with Angus Paint

Use your finger as your painting tool. Paint with any color of the rainbow. Paint with different brush sizes. Save your paintings to your device. Paint upon a picture from your device. Paint in portrait or landscape mode. Have fun, and please rate on Google Play!
A new version of Solitaire invented by Angus Software
The objective of the game is to score as many points as possible before the deck runs out of cards.
Read at an enhanced speed with Blink Reader

Blink Reader is an Application for the Android mobile Operating System. The purpose of the Blink Reader app is to provide its users with an Application for reading text at an enhanced speed. This enhanced reading speed is achieved by displaying one word of the text which the user is reading at a time. By displaying one word at a time, the user doesn't have to move their eyes, and can manually control the words per minute at which the text displays.
Improve your touching and pressing aim with this targeting game
With four different modes to help improve you accuracy and speed, Tap Target Booster is fun way to measure and hone your touch screen skills!
There are no new Posts to display.
